vulnerability.title: Enable Dracut FIPS Module
vulnerability.description: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 has an installation-time kernel flag that can enable FIPS mode. The installer must be booted with fips=1 for the system to have FIPS mode enabled. Enabling FIPS mode on a preexisting system is not supported. If this rule fails on an installed system, then this is a permanent finding and cannot be fixed. To enable FIPS, the system requires that the fips module is added in dracut configuration. Check if /etc/dracut.conf.d/40-fips.conf contain add_dracutmodules+=" fips "
vulnerability.recommendation: Review and remediate: Enable Dracut FIPS Module

triage_decision.reason: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 installation-time kernel flag can enable FIPS mode when booted with fips=1. Without this flag, FIPS mode is not enabled on preexisting systems, leading to a permanent finding.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; FIPS mode not enabled; Missing fips=1 kernel boot parameter; /etc/dracut.conf.d/40-fips.conf missing add_dracutmodules+= fips

execution_details.command: echo YWRkX2RyYWN1dG1vZHVsZXMrPSIgZmlwcyAi | base64 -d > /etc/dracut.conf.d/40-fips.conf.tmp && chmod append /etc/dracut.conf.d/40-fips.conf.tmp && mv /etc/dracut.conf.d/40-fips.conf.tmp /etc/dracut.conf.d/40-fips.conf
execution_details.stderr:
chmod: invalid mode: ‘append’
Try 'chmod --help' for more information.
